name: Sierra Lidón — circular desde Monreal del Campo (BTT03)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Monreal Del Campo offers diverse landscapes, from significant natural wetlands to reforested hills and varied terrain. The region features rolling hills and some mountainous sections, providing a dynamic backdrop for outdoor pursuits. It is also integrated into historical routes like El Camino del Cid, blending cultural heritage with scenic beauty. Best mountain bike trails around Monreal Del Campo The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra Lidón — circular desde Monreal del Campo (BTT03), a 38.5 miles (61.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Sierra Lidón area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ojos de Monreal del Río Jiloca — ida y vuelta desde Villafranca del Campo, an easy 18.7 miles (30.0 km) path. This route features the unique Ojos de Monreal wetland area. Local mountain bikers also love the Monreal del Campo – Caño el Gato loop from Monreal del Campo, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Monreal Del Campo is defined by significant natural wetlands, reforested areas like El Monte, and historical routes such as El Camino del Cid. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to moderate mountain bike trails. The routes in Monreal Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from 4 reviews. 64 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Monreal Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Monreal Del Campo?
A: There are currently 13 mountain bike trails documented around Monreal Del Campo on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy rides to more challenging moderate trails.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there is one easy mountain bike trail. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004903398/ojos-de-monreal-del-rio-jiloca-ida-y-vuelta-desde-villafranca-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos de Monreal del Río Jiloca — ida y vuelta desde Villafranca del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 30.0 km path that features the unique Ojos de Monreal wetland area, making it a great option for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Monreal Del Campo?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, including significant natural wetlands like the Ojos de Monreal, reforested areas such as El Monte, and varied landscapes with rolling hills and some mountainous sections. You'll find a dynamic backdrop for your rides, often integrated into historical routes like El Camino del Cid.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, many trails in Monreal Del Campo are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003685330/sierra-lidon-circular-desde-monreal-del-campo-btt03" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Lidón — circular desde Monreal del Campo (BTT03)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 61.9 km circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monreal del Campo – Caño el Gato loop from Monreal del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 48.3 km.

Q: What are some notable natural features or attractions I can see while mountain biking?
A: You can explore significant natural wetlands like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos de Monreal&lt;/a&gt;, which are large groundwater discharge springs. The area also features reforested hills of El Monte, offering pleasant paths. Historically, the region is part of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2530689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, blending cultural heritage with scenic beauty.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Monreal Del Campo?
A: The mountain bike routes in Monreal Del Campo are generally well-regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.0 stars from 4 reviews. 64 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the blend of natural and historical elements.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult mountain bike routes in Monreal Del Campo?
A: Within the immediate Monreal Del Campo area, the komoot guide primarily lists moderate trails, with no routes explicitly classified as 'difficult'. However, the wider region, including nearby Maestrazgo Mountain Bike Center, offers a broader range of difficulties, including black (very difficult) and red (difficult) trails for experienced riders.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Monreal Del Campo?
A: Monreal del Campo is suitable for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. While winter mountain biking is possible, it's advisable to check local weather conditions as some portions might require pushing your bike due to conditions.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: The diverse terrain, including rolling hills and mountainous sections, naturally offers many scenic spots. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monreal del Campo – Hilltop House loop from Monreal del Campo&lt;/a&gt; are likely to provide elevated views. The reforested areas of El Monte also offer pleasant natural environments.

Q: How can I get to Monreal Del Campo for mountain biking?
A: Monreal del Campo is accessible by road. The region's integration into larger cycling initiatives like 'El Camino del Cid' and the 'Santander-Mediterráneo Nature Trail' suggests good connectivity. For specific public transport options, it's best to check local and regional transport schedules.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for endurance riders?
A: Yes, for endurance riders, there are several longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003685330/sierra-lidon-circular-desde-monreal-del-campo-btt03" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Lidón — circular desde Monreal del Campo (BTT03)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 61.9 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Poyo del Cid – Embalse de Lechago loop from Torrijo del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is even longer at 75.37 km, offering a significant challenge.

Q: What historical sites can I explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Monreal del Campo is a key point along &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2530689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, a literary and historical route. You can also find historical settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozuel del Campo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1263070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafranca del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of this rich cultural heritage.
tips: Next to Cerro de la Cruz and a few kilometers from Villafranca del Campo, we find these beautiful views that will accompany us during a good part of our route if we head towards the Sierra Lidón
Beautiful picture that the Sierra de Lidón gives us while we walk one of its paths. A passing area of Route 3 of the BTT Center of the Jiloca region.

Caño el Gato is a ravine through which the waters flow from the Sierra de Rubielos to the town of Monreal del Campo, flowing into the Jiloca. We will ride a mountain bike between rocky limestone walls that give us a beautiful picture. If we approach the place, we will find an information panel that will tell us an interesting legend of the place.
